etymology.description: semantic 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 睪(睪) -> concept of "marsh, wetland" by combining water with phonetic
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") suggests a watery area, while the phonetic component 睪(睪) contributes the pronunciation. The combination reflects a place characterized by presence of water, leading to senses like "marsh" and "wetland". The extension to "benefit" and "grace" links metaphorically to the idea of water providing sustenance and nurturing qualities.
etymology.simplification: Simplified 泽(澤) replaces 睪(睪) with simplified 𦰩, maintaining the pronunciation but reducing strokes.

parts.english: a kind of examination; to watch over

examples.chinese: 在雨后的清晨(在雨後的清晨)，我们穿过了一片广阔的泽地(我們穿過了一片廣闊的澤地)。
examples.pinyin: Zài yǔhòu de qīngchén, wǒmen chuānguòle yī piàn guǎngkuò de zédì.
examples.english: On the morning after the rain, we walked through a vast marshland.

examples.chinese: 他的恩泽(他的恩澤)让所有人都感激不已(讓所有人都感激不已)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de ēnzé ràng suǒyǒu rén dōu gǎnjī bù yǐ.
examples.english: His grace made everyone feel incredibly grateful.

examples.chinese: 这片湿地(這片濕地)在春天成为鸟类的天堂(在春天成為鳥類的天堂)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè piàn shīdì zài chūntiān chéngwéi niǎolèi de tiāntáng.
examples.english: This wetland becomes a paradise for birds in the spring.
